mission.title: Install and Configure Runme on Kubernetes
mission.description: Runme makes runbooks actually runnable by executing code snippets directly from markdown files. It supports Shell/Bash, Python, Ruby, JavaScript, and many other runtimes. Runme can be used as a CLI, VS Code extension, or integrated into Kubernetes workflows for operational documentation.

steps.title: Install the Runme CLI
steps.description:
Install the Runme CLI using Homebrew, NPM, or by downloading a binary.
```bash
# Option 1: Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install runme

# Option 2: NPM
npm install -g runme

# Option 3: Go install
go install github.com/runmedev/runme/v3@main

# Verify installation
runme --version
```

steps.title: List available runbook tasks
steps.description:
Use runme to discover runnable code blocks in your markdown documentation.
```bash
runme list
```

steps.title: Run a task from a markdown file
steps.description:
Execute a named code block from a README or runbook.
```bash
runme run <task-name>
```

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes runbook
steps.description:
Create a markdown runbook with Kubernetes operations that Runme can execute.
```bash
cat <<'EOF' > k8s-runbook.md
# Kubernetes Operations Runbook

## Check cluster health

\`\`\`bash {name=check-health}
kubectl get nodes
kubectl get pods --all-namespaces | grep -v Running
\`\`\`

## Deploy application

\`\`\`bash {name=deploy-app}
kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app
\`\`\`
EOF
```

steps.title: Run the Kubernetes runbook
steps.description:
Execute specific tasks from the runbook.
```bash
runme run check-health --filename k8s-runbook.md
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Runme CLI available for running code blocks from markdown files. Runme transforms operational documentation into executable runbooks, reducing errors from copy-paste and ensuring documentation stays current.

uninstall.title: Remove the Runme CLI
uninstall.description: Uninstall via Homebrew (brew uninstall runme), NPM (npm uninstall -g runme), or delete the binary.

upgrade.title: Update Runme
upgrade.description: Upgrade via your package manager: brew upgrade runme or npm update -g runme.

troubleshooting.title: Code blocks not detected
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure code blocks use fenced syntax (triple backticks) with a language identifier and name attribute.
```bash
runme list --filename your-file.md
```

troubleshooting.title: Environment variables not persisting
troubleshooting.description: Runme preserves environment variables across cell executions within the same session. Ensure you're running tasks in the same session.

troubleshooting.title: Permission denied errors
troubleshooting.description: Check that the scripts in your markdown have appropriate permissions and the runme binary is in your PATH.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed. Runme can be installed via Homebrew, NPM, or as a Go binary. A VS Code extension is also available for an interactive notebook experience.
